description: The sun is a relentless judge over a land of dust and tumbleweeds. Between the saloon doors and the lone trail, a gunslinger's honor is the only law that truly matters. Ride into the sunset of your own tale.
endings: They rode off into the sunset, leaving their troubles behind.
The outlaw was caught, and peace returned to the town.
The gold was returned, and the ranch was saved.
A new sheriff pinned on the star, ready for the next dawn.
The duel was over, and the dust finally settled on Main Street.
They found their home at the end of the long trail.
The train steamed away, carrying the secrets of the West.
The campfire died out, and the stars watched over the plains.
Justice was swift and final under the desert sun.
The two families finally ended their long feud.
The hidden canyon remained a secret forever.
They traded their guns for a plow and a piece of land.
The legend of the lone rider lived on in the songs of the saloon.
The bank vault was empty, and the trail went cold.
The desert bloom greeted them as the spring rains came.
They were buried on the hill where the wild roses grow.
The frontier moved on, but their story remained in the wind.
The last bullet found its mark, and the war was won.
They built a town where there once was only sand.
And the wild west became a memory in the books of history.
